questions.question_text: A computer costs $\$800$ before GST. Find the price including GST.

options.why: Added $\$8$ instead of $10\%$ of $\$800$, which is $\$80$.
Subtracted the GST instead of adding it to the pre-GST price.
Divided by $1.1$ as if $\$800$ already included GST.

questions.question_text: A lounge costs $\$990$ including GST. Find the pre-GST price.

options.why: Took $10\%$ off $\$990$ instead of dividing by $1.1$; the base is not the inclusive price.
Added $10\%$ to $\$990$ instead of removing the GST already included.
Found the GST amount ($\$990 \div 11$) rather than the pre-GST price.

questions.question_text: A trader buys goods for $\$80$ and sells them for $\$100$. Find the percentage profit.

options.why: Divided the $\$20$ profit by the selling price instead of the cost price.
Divided the cost price by the selling price rather than using the profit.
Called the change a loss even though the selling price is higher than the cost.

questions.question_text: An investor buys shares for $\$8000$ and sells them for $\$6300$. Find the percentage profit or loss.

options.why: Divided the $\$1700$ loss by the selling price instead of the cost price.
Ignored that the selling price is lower than the cost, so it is a loss.
Used $\$1700$ as a percentage of $\$10000$ rather than of the cost price.

questions.question_text: A car dealer makes a $25\%$ profit by selling a car for $\$4000$. How much did she pay for the car?

options.why: Took $25\%$ off $\$4000$ instead of dividing by $125\%$; the selling price is not $100\%$.
Added $25\%$ to $\$4000$ instead of working back to the cost price.
Found $25\%$ of $\$4000$, the profit, rather than the cost price.

questions.question_text: Aimee has two quotes for car repairs. The first is $\$2450$ before GST. The second is $\$2680$ including GST. Which quote is cheaper?

options.text: The second, by $\$15$, since the first comes to $\$2695$ including GST.
The first, by $\$230$, comparing $\$2450$ with $\$2680$ directly.
options.why: Compares a pre-GST price with a GST-inclusive price without adding GST to the first.

options.text: The second, by $\$268$, after taking GST off the second quote.
options.why: Removes GST from the second quote instead of adding it to the first for a fair comparison.

options.text: They cost the same once GST is added to both.
options.why: Only the first quote needs GST added; the second already includes it.

questions.solution_text:
First quote, including GST: $\$2450 \times 1.1 = \$2695$.
Second quote, including GST: $\$2680$.
$\$2695 - \$2680 = \$15$
The second quote is cheaper by $\$15$.
